findingsNew.claim: The create agent remains on the old CAPABILITY SCAN heading while the rest of the exposed agent mirror set has moved to ROUTING SCAN, leaving an AI-facing runtime surface inconsistent with the ADR-005 cleanup.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Searched the scoped agent directory for CAPABILITY SCAN and ROUTING SCAN; only create.md retained the old heading among scanned routing sections. Checked root runtime mirrors to confirm @create is an exposed AI-facing agent, not a private orphan.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The create agent may have been added after the original audit list, but .opencode/agents/ is in the review scope and root mirrors advertise @create, so it inherits the same public-surface vocabulary requirement.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if maintainers explicitly classify .opencode/agents/create.md as outside AI-facing public-surface cleanup despite the root mirrors exposing @create, or if capability vocabulary is intentionally allowed for agent routing headings.

agent_cross_runtime.notes: Root runtime mirrors expose @create, but the .opencode/agents/create.md prompt was not synchronized with the agent heading vocabulary used by the rest of the prompt set.

spec_code.notes: ADR-005/workflow-invariance cleanup requires public AI-facing surfaces to avoid private taxonomy vocabulary, and this AI-facing prompt still uses the old capability heading.

resource_map_coverage.notes: No applied/T-*.md files were present to cross-check; direct scope audit shows the resource-map agent table omits an exposed agent file under the scoped .opencode/agents/ directory.

overlay.notes: Agent mirrors are mostly synchronized, but the create-agent mirror remains stale.

findingDetails.title: Create agent prompt was missed by the cross-runtime agent vocabulary cleanup
findingDetails.evidence: The resource map says all AI-facing agent definitions under .opencode/agents/ are in scope for the heading rename, but omits create.md from the enumerated agent cleanup list. AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md expose @create as a first-class agent, and create.md still contains ## 2. CAPABILITY SCAN while sibling agent prompts use ROUTING SCAN.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rename the create.md section heading to ROUTING SCAN and add .opencode/agents/create.md to the agent cleanup/resource-map coverage so future mirror scans include it.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Directory-wide heading scan found the old heading in create.md and routing headings in the other sampled agent prompts; root instruction mirrors confirm the missed file is reachable through the same agent surface.

title: Create agent prompt was missed by the cross-runtime agent vocabulary cleanup

detail: Classified as P1 because the stale heading is in an exposed AI-facing agent prompt and contradicts the resource-map/ADR-005 public-surface cleanup, but it does not independently create data loss or security impact.

name: agent_cross_runtime
detail: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md expose @create, while .opencode/agents/create.md still uses the pre-cleanup CAPABILITY SCAN heading unlike sibling agent prompts.

direction: Treat create.md as private or out-of-scope
reason: Root runtime instruction mirrors advertise @create, and .opencode/agents/ is included as a scoped review directory.
